home.metadataDescription: Terminal-styled member profiles for Discord: marketplace templates, gallery, blog, analytics, vouches, and Premium add-ons. Sign in with Discord.
home.intro: Terminal-styled member profiles for Discord communities: templates, gallery, blog, analytics, and more.

about.metadataDescription: {siteName}: terminal-styled member profiles, Discord or local sign-in (SRP + passkeys), marketplace, gallery, blog, and more.
about.title: About {siteName}
about.subtitle: {siteDescription}
about.whatItIsBody: {siteName} is a member profile site with a terminal-inspired look and deep customization: themes, effects, widgets, and optional community features like vouches and leaderboards. It is built for people who already live on Discord and want a profile that matches that vibe—not a generic link-in-bio block.
about.marketplaceItem: <marketplace>Marketplace</marketplace> — browse and apply community-made profile templates.
about.galleryBlogItem: Profile gallery, paste, and markdown blog at /yourname/blog.
about.dashboardItem: Short links, analytics, badges, Roblox and Discord widgets, and more from the dashboard after you sign in.
about.premiumBody: Extra visual effects, expanded limits, and add-ons are available through <premium>Premium</premium> (Polar checkout). Pricing is shown in the dashboard after you sign in—plans and products can change, so the dashboard always reflects what is live.
about.privacyBody: You can sign in with Discord OAuth or a local account (email verification, SRP-6a password, optional WebAuthn passkeys). We store what you add to your profile. Profile view analytics use basic technical data (for example IP and user agent) as described in our <privacyPolicy>Privacy Policy</privacyPolicy>. We do not sell your data.
about.discordLocalBody: Sign in with Discord OAuth, or create a local account with a unique username, verified email, and SRP-6a password (the server stores a verifier only). You can add WebAuthn passkeys after sign-in. Social-only providers beyond Discord are not a focus right now.
about.customDomainsBody: Hosting your profile on your own domain (CNAME/apex) or offering registrar-style DNS is out of scope for now. Profiles live on dread.lol/yourname. We may revisit custom domains later; it is not on the public roadmap today.
about.apiBody: A subset of HTTP routes is documented for public reads—see <docs>Documentation</docs> (including <docsApi>HTTP API scope</docsApi>). Everything else is for the web app and may change without notice.
about.helpBody: For questions or issues, use whichever support or community channel you already use for {siteName}, or reach out through contacts listed on the site when available.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.acceptanceBody: By using {siteName}, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.
terms.useOfServiceBody: You must use the service in good faith. Do not abuse, spam, or attempt to harm the service or other users. Your profile and content must comply with applicable laws and not infringe others’ rights.
terms.accountAccessBody: Access may require approval. We may suspend or remove access if we believe you have violated these terms or for operational reasons.
terms.changesBody: We may update these terms. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance. Important changes may be communicated via the service or community.
terms.contactBody: Questions? Contact us through the service or community linked from the site.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.collectBody: We use Discord OAuth to let you sign in. We store your Discord user ID, username, display name, and avatar URL. If you create a member profile, we store the information you add (bio, links, etc.) and basic view logs (IP, user agent, timestamp) for your profile page.
privacyPage.howWeUseBody: Your data is used to run the service: authentication, displaying your profile, and (for admins) approving accounts and managing badges.
privacyPage.sharingBody: We do not sell your data. We may share data only as required by law or to protect the service.
privacyPage.contactBody: Questions? Contact us through the service or community linked from the site.
